title: contextBlocks single-item object form depth field is stored but render_recursive is not auto-applied
description: Object-form contextBlocks entries `{ name, item, depth }` at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/compile.ts line 460 wrap the referenced item via wrapItemContent (line 180+) — raw JSON inside a context_block XML tag. The depth field is preserved on the injected array element (compile.ts line 469: `depth: ref.depth ?? 0`) as metadata but does NOT auto-trigger render_recursive. Template authors must explicitly call `render_recursive(resolve(id), _<name>_depth)` to get the per-item-macro rendered view that the depth field promises. The depth metadata exists but is plumbing-only; rendering still requires explicit author action.

evidence.reference: FB-011 'contextBlocks single-item injection is raw JSON — render_recursive must be called explicitly'; actual_behavior: _requirements_item contains raw JSON wrapped in context_block XML; _requirements_depth contains the specified depth; template must call render_recursive to get macro-rendered output

Investigation report FB-011 section confirms both surfaces verbatim (compile.ts:460 wrapItemContent invocation; compile.ts:469 depth metadata storage; compile.ts:180-187 wrapItemContent raw-JSON wrapping); verdict 'Tool surface' — modify compile.ts to detect depth > 0 on object-form entries and wrap returned item with render_recursive invocation
wrapItemContent wraps as raw JSON; depth: ref.depth ?? 0 stored as metadata only — no render_recursive auto-application

impact: Authors expecting depth > 0 to produce a pre-rendered, macro-formatted view receive raw JSON instead. The per-item rendering infrastructure (FEAT-001 array_key column, CANONICAL_MACRO_NAMES registry) is built but the most natural ergonomic — 'I asked for depth 2, I expect a 2-deep recursive render' — requires manual template glue.
proposed_resolution: Tool surface at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/compile.ts line 460 (wrapItemContent invocation site) and line 469 (array element shape): when ref.depth > 0, wrap the returned item with a render_recursive call invoking the kind's per-item macro at the declared depth, instead of storing raw JSON with depth as unused metadata. Bare-string contextBlocks form remains unchanged (no item, no depth semantic). Sibling to FGAP-161 fix for FB-010 — the auto-render behavior would also be available to bare-string projection per FGAP-161's `depth` extension.
